# SC-02: Stage Directive Injection

**Track:** Security Controls (`SC`) · **Priority:** MUST (mandatory) · **Status:** draft

Inject the signed, immutable stage directive bundle into the agent context at the entry of each A-SDLC stage. Stage directives layer on top of the core directives (SC-01) and add behavioural constraints specific to the work performed in that stage. The agent must acknowledge receipt before beginning stage work. Runs once per stage entry across all six stages.

## Required In Stages

| Stage | Name | Applicability |
|-------|------|---------------|
| 1 | Intent Ingestion | Mandatory |
| 2 | System Design | Mandatory |
| 3 | Coding & Implementation | Mandatory |
| 4 | Testing & Documentation | Mandatory |
| 5 | Deployment & Release | Mandatory |
| 6 | Observability & Maintenance | Mandatory |

## Delegation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Pattern** | Fully automated (signed) |
| **Agent does** | At each stage entry, receive and acknowledge the signed stage directive payload for that stage. Log acknowledgement with stage number, timestamp, and agent identifier before performing any stage work. |
| **Human does** | Security Architect authors, signs, and publishes each stage directive payload. Reviews directive violation logs periodically. Triggers re-injection after any directive update. |

## Inputs

| Artifact | Source Control | Description |
|----------|---------------|-------------|
| Stage directive payload | Signed YAML stage directive bundle from directives/stages/0N-name.yaml, where N is the stage number being entered. |  |


## Outputs

| Artifact | Type | Description | Retention |
|----------|------|-------------|-----------|
| Stage directive injection confirmation | evidence | Confirmation that the agent received and acknowledged the stage directive payload, with stage number, timestamp, and agent identifier. | 7 years (DORA) |


## Automation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Frequency** | per_stage_entry |
| **SLA** | — |
| **Tooling** | — |

## Failure Behaviour

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **On failure** | block |
| **Escalate to** | Security Architect |

If stage directive injection fails or the agent does not acknowledge, the stage cannot begin. The session must be restarted from directive injection.

## Dependencies

- `SC-01`


## Regulatory Mapping

| Regulation | Article | Obligation |
|------------|---------|------------|
| DORA | Art. 9(4)(c) | ICT security policies and access controls must govern automated agents |
| EU AI Act | Art. 14(1) | Human oversight measures must be built into AI system design and operation |


## Ownership

**Owner team:** Security · **Owner role:** Security Architect
